XALLOC_ATTRIBUTES

Specifies memory allocation attributes. Used with the memory allocation replacement functions (XMemAlloc, XMemFree, and XMemSize).

typedef struct _XALLOC_ATTRIBUTES{
    DWORD dwObjectType : 13;
    DWORD dwHeapTracksAttributes : 1;
    DWORD dwMustSucceed : 1;
    DWORD dwFixedSize : 1;
    DWORD dwAllocatorId : 8;
    DWORD dwAlignment : 4;
    DWORD dwMemoryProtect : 2;
    DWORD dwZeroInitialize : 1;
    DWORD dwMemoryType : 1;
} XALLOC_ATTRIBUTES, *PXALLOC_ATTRIBUTES;

Members

dwObjectType
Specifies a library or component defined value that identifies the object type being allocated. A heap allocator might use this information, in conjunction with the allocation size, to minimize fragmentation (by grouping similar object allocations) and to increase performance (by preallocating memory chunks and performing simple array-style allocations for objects).
dwHeapTracksAttributes
Specifies whether the heap is responsible for tracking the allocation attributes for this allocation. Specify TRUE to have the heap track allocation attributes, FALSE otherwise.
dwMustSucceed
Specifies whether this allocation must succeed (as the calling library can not handle a failure case), or whether the allocation is not critical and the calling library can properly handle a failed allocation. Specify TRUE for an allocation that must succeed, FALSE otherwise.
dwFixedSize
Specifies whether allocations of this type (as specified by dwObjectType) will always be the same size. Specify TRUE if allocations of this type will always be the same size, and FALSE otherwise. This information is useful for heap routines to minimizing fragmentation.
dwAllocatorId
Specifies an identifier unique to each library or component that performs memory allocations. Titles may use values between 0 and 127 to specify title components. Values in the range 128 to 191 are used by system and XTL components. Values in the range 192 to 255 are used by middleware components. See XALLOC_ALLOCATOR_IDS.
dwAlignment

Specifies the byte alignment of the allocated memory.

For heap allocations, dwAlignment must be one of the following values. Allocations from XTL libraries will specify 16-byte alignment for this value. Alignments greater than 16 bytes are not supported. Titles may perform allocations on 4 or 8 byte boundaries if their heap code supports those alignments.

Value Description
XALLOC_ALIGNMENT_DEFAULT default alignment (16-byte)
XALLOC_ALIGNMENT_4 4-byte alignment
XALLOC_ALIGNMENT_8 8-byte alignment
XALLOC_ALIGNMENT_16 16-byte alignment

For physical memory allocations, dwAlignment must be one of the following values.

Value Description
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_DEFAULT default alignment (4 KB)
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_4K_BELOW_16M 4 KB alignment, memory below 16 MB
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_4 4-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_8 8-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_16 16-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_32 32-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_64 64-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_128 128-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_256 256-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_512 512-byte alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_1K 1 KB alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_2K 2 KB alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_4K 4 KB alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_8K 8 KB alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_16K 16 KB alignment
XALLOC_PHYSICAL_ALIGNMENT_32K 32 KB alignment

dwMemoryProtect

Specifies the access protection and optional memory type modifiers. One of the following access protection flags must be specified:
Value Description
XALLOC_MEMPROTECT_READONLY Enables read and execute access to the committed region of pages. An attempt to write to the committed region results in an access violation.
XALLOC_MEMPROTECT_READWRITE Enables read, write, and execute access to the committed region of pages."

In addition, one of the following flags may be specified to modify the characteristics of the allocated memory:

Value Description
XALLOC_MEMPROTECT_NOCACHE Allows no memory caching for memory in the committed region of pages. The hardware attributes for the physical memory will be treated as "no cache." This is useful, for example, for mapping a video frame buffer with no caching.
XALLOC_MEMPROTECT_WRITECOMBINE The allocated memory has a write-combining cache policy. Write combining is a special caching mode that batches writes to the same cache line so they can be transferred in a single bus clock. Write combining does not preserve ordering of the writes, a tradeoff that is usually acceptable for frame buffers.

dwZeroInitialize
Specifies whether the memory should be zero-initialized when allocated. Specify TRUE to zero-initialize the memory, FALSE otherwise.
dwMemoryType
Specifies the memory type. Can be one of the following values:
Value Description
XALLOC_MEMTYPE_HEAP Heap (virtual) memory
XALLOC_MEMTYPE_PHYSICAL Physical memory

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xbox.h; for include use xtl.h.

See Also

XMemAlloc, XMemFree, XMemSize, XSetAttributesOnHeapAlloc, XGetAttributesOnHeapAlloc, XALLOC_ALLOCATOR_IDS
